description_long: This gatehouse is a robust stone structure with a rectangular main building and an attached walled courtyard. The main building features a series of evenly spaced vertical sections, each topped with a sloped, metallic-looking roof, and a central tower capped with a conical roof. The stone walls are thick and punctuated by small, narrow windows, suggesting a defensive purpose. The entryway is a prominent arched opening at the center of the front facade, leading directly into the courtyard, which is enclosed by a lower stone wall with regularly spaced pillars. The overall appearance is utilitarian and fortified, with minimal decorative elements, emphasizing function over form. The color palette is dominated by light stone and dark roofing, giving it a historic, medieval character.
description: A fortified stone gatehouse with a central arched entry, conical-roofed tower, and an attached walled courtyard, featuring thick walls and minimal windows.
description_view_0: The main building and attached courtyard are visible, with the conical-roofed tower and sloped roofs prominent; the courtyard wall is seen extending to the right.
description_view_1: The arched entryway is centered and clearly visible, with the courtyard wall forming a rectangular enclosure in the foreground.
description_view_2: The rear and side of the gatehouse are visible, showing the solid stone construction and the courtyard wall extending to the left.
description_view_3: The back of the gatehouse is shown, highlighting the series of sloped roofs and the conical tower, with the courtyard wall partially visible.
attribute_entryway_design: arched stone doorway with a prominent central opening
attribute_window_placement: small, narrow windows spaced along the upper walls
